summary.what: Add provenance classifier as last step in skip chain
summary.why: Phase 3 of v0.23.0 plan. The provenance classifier is what makes the gate provenance-aware — but landing it as a standalone, fully tested unit (without yet wiring it into the gate decision) means the precedence ordering and safe-degradation behavior are locked in by tests independent of the gate-path wiring that comes in phase 5. Smaller, safer commit; the change is observable only through classifyCommit's --explain surface, not through the gate.
summary.how: Created internal/ledger/provenance.go with a ProvenanceConfig struct and a pure classifyByProvenance function. Extended internal/ledger/storage.go's Storage struct with a provenance field (zero-valued = disabled). Threaded the classifier into classifyCommit at the END of the chain (after infra → identity → content) so existing reasons preserve their decision-relevance — a documented or acked commit doesn't suddenly relabel as foreign-author just because the email differs. Wrote provenance_test.go: 9 pure-function cases (every combination of email × staleness, plus safe-degradation paths for empty UserEmail, zero window, zero CommitDate, future date) and 4 precedence subtests that lock in earlier-wins ordering against a foreign+stale commit.

notes: Considered passing ProvenanceConfig as a parameter to classifyCommit instead of holding it on Storage — rejected because it bloats the call signature in every caller (filterByRules, classifyCommit, traceFilterDecisions, ExplainPending) and the config has the same lifecycle as the Storage (per-repo, loaded at construction). Considered shipping with provenance NOT in the chain yet — rejected because it leaves the precedence order untested until phase 5; landing it now with empty config means the chain order is verified independently. Did NOT include a 'time-source clock injection' pattern; ProvenanceConfig.Now is just a time.Time field that callers populate (production with time.Now(), tests with a fixed instant). The test using a fixed Now=2026-06-01 noon is reproducible without any clock plumbing.
